image:image.image:title: Level Up IRL: Designing Your Purpose
image:image.image:caption: Level Up IRL: Designing Your Purpose: Teams care about the topic, but the surrounding systems still produce activity without durable capability, better decisions, or visible business impact.

image:image.image:title: Failed Game Becomes a Hit - 8 Years Later
image:image.image:caption: Failed Game Becomes a Hit - 8 Years Later: Teams are treating AI adoption as tooling rollout, but the real constraint is whether work systems, manager behavior, and capability-building routines actually change.

image:image.image:title: Why Your Game Mechanic Is the Learning

image:image.image:title: From Skeptics to Superstars: The Power of Play at Work
image:image.image:caption: From Skeptics to Superstars: The Power of Play at Work: Teams care about the topic, but the surrounding systems still produce activity without durable capability, better decisions, or visible business impact.

image:image.image:title: Civic Engagement Through Classroom Games
image:image.image:caption: Civic Engagement Through Classroom Games: Teams use visible mechanics, but the design still fails when it does not protect motivation, identity, and repeat behavior in real work.

image:image.image:title: The capability decisions that make AI useful
image:image.image:caption: The capability decisions that make AI useful: Teams are treating AI adoption as tooling rollout, but the real constraint is whether work systems, manager behavior, and capability-building routines actually change.

image:image.image:title: AI adoption fails when it feels like control
image:image.image:caption: AI adoption fails when it feels like control: Teams are treating AI adoption as tooling rollout, but the real constraint is whether work systems, manager behavior, and capability-building routines actually change.

image:image.image:title: Escape Rooms for Growth
image:image.image:caption: Escape Rooms for Growth: Teams care about the topic, but the surrounding systems still produce activity without durable capability, better decisions, or visible business impact.

image:image.image:title: Why activity metrics are not evidence of impact
image:image.image:caption: Why activity metrics are not evidence of impact: Teams care about the topic, but the surrounding systems still produce activity without durable capability, better decisions, or visible business impact.

image:image.image:title: The One Choice That Will Determine Your Online Success
image:image.image:caption: The One Choice That Will Determine Your Online Success: Teams care about the topic, but the surrounding systems still produce activity without durable capability, better decisions, or visible business impact.

image:image.image:title: From Fan to Creator
image:image.image:caption: From Fan to Creator: Teams are treating AI adoption as tooling rollout, but the real constraint is whether work systems, manager behavior, and capability-building routines actually change.

image:image.image:title: Epic Fight? Here’s What Really Happened
image:image.image:caption: Epic Fight? Here’s What Really Happened: Teams care about the topic, but the surrounding systems still produce activity without durable capability, better decisions, or visible business impact.

image:image.image:title: From Viral Spike to Sticky Play
image:image.image:caption: From Viral Spike to Sticky Play: Teams care about the topic, but the surrounding systems still produce activity without durable capability, better decisions, or visible business impact.

image:image.image:title: The Secret Framework That Keeps Users Coming Back
image:image.image:caption: The Secret Framework That Keeps Users Coming Back: Teams care about the topic, but the surrounding systems still produce activity without durable capability, better decisions, or visible business impact.

image:image.image:title: Shared quests can strengthen academic teams
image:image.image:caption: Shared quests can strengthen academic teams: Teams care about the topic, but the surrounding systems still produce activity without durable capability, better decisions, or visible business impact.

image:image.image:title: 8 Years Building the Global Leading Gamification
image:image.image:caption: 8 Years Building the Global Leading Gamification : Teams use visible mechanics, but the design still fails when it does not protect motivation, identity, and repeat behavior in real work.

image:image.image:title: Building Beats Playing
image:image.image:caption: Building Beats Playing: Teams care about the topic, but the surrounding systems still produce activity without durable capability, better decisions, or visible business impact.

image:image.image:title: Effort isn't the problem. Your learning system is
image:image.image:caption: Most learning failures are system failures: passive methods, weak feedback, and no spaced retrieval.

image:image.image:title: Design thinking is not retention
image:image.image:caption: Design thinking improves usability. Retention needs a behaviour model that makes people return.

image:image.image:title: Learning games fail when we try to teach everything
image:image.image:caption: Learning games break when they try to teach everything. Design around one high value behaviour, then scale.

image:image.image:title: Delight belongs in the core loop
image:image.image:caption: Delight is not garnish. Research on user experience shows that appeal comes from the interplay of pragmatic and hedonic quality during use, not from decorative extras added at the end.

image:image.image:title: Novelty is not retention
image:image.image:caption: Gamification can help people start, but it rarely keeps them by itself. Longitudinal research shows that once novelty fades, retention depends on meaningful progress, tailored design, and autonomy.

image:image.image:title: Why teams reject AI that feels like replacement
image:image.image:caption: AI adoption usually fails for a human reason. When the system feels like control or replacement, teams resist it. When it supports autonomy, competence, and visible wins, usage rises.

image:image.image:title: When a leaderboard becomes surveillance
image:image.image:caption: Leaderboards can create short-term movement, but in workplace learning they often drift into surveillance. The better design question is whether the system strengthens autonomy, competence, and useful feedback.
